My experience with Vodafone has been extremely disappointing from the day I entered into my contract. The network service has been consistently unstable, with frequent signal dropouts and connectivity issues regardless of whether the handset displays signal bars or not. This has made the service unreliable and, at times, unusable.

What is particularly frustrating is the lack of accountability from Vodafone. Every complaint is met with the same repetitive responses: being told that the issue is related to my location, my handset, or that I should check for ongoing network works. Rather than addressing the underlying problem, it feels as though customers are being led through a scripted process designed to delay resolution and discourage complaints.

The reality is that the network performance has been poor since day one, and I have yet to receive any meaningful acknowledgement or apology for the ongoing disruption. Customers pay for a service that should meet a reasonable standard of reliability, and in my case Vodafone has failed to deliver that standard.

I have reached the point where enough is enough. Excuses and deflections are not solutions. Vodafone needs to acknowledge the instability of its network, take responsibility for the service it is providing, and implement a permanent fix. If this issue is not resolved promptly, I will have no option but to cancel my contract and take my concerns further.

I intend to share this experience publicly because prospective customers deserve to know the level of service they may receive. Vodafone must do better and start treating customer concerns seriously rather than repeatedly shifting blame elsewhere.
Really helpful visit today thanks to Mia, sorted out a problem that Doncaster branch could not solve 👍

Trustpilot should start 0 star as an option after using Vodafone,already poor connection then this happens

I am extremely disappointed with Vodafone’s service and the way my home move request has been handled.

I have been a Vodafone broadband customer and recently moved to a new home. Before moving, I checked Vodafone’s coverage checker, which showed that my area was covered by Vodafone Full Fibre. Although my exact house number was not appearing, all neighbouring properties in the same development were listed as eligible for Vodafone Full Fibre broadband.

Since my property is a newly built home, I assumed the issue was simply that my address had not yet been fully updated in the system. When I contacted Vodafone’s chat support, I was told that the problem was likely related to my address not yet being updated through Royal Mail. Based on that explanation, I trusted Vodafone and believed that transferring my broadband service would not be a problem.

I even checked the Openreach availability checker, where my address is correctly listed. Openreach confirms that Full Fibre is available at my property and shows Vodafone as one of the providers available at my address. This gave me even more confidence that my existing broadband service could simply be transferred.

However, when I contacted Vodafone by phone, I was given completely different information. I spoke to an advisor named Mariam, who told me that Vodafone does not provide broadband service to my area and that there was nothing she could do. This made absolutely no sense given the information shown by Vodafone’s own coverage checker, the advice previously given by Vodafone’s chat support, and the information available through Openreach.

When I asked why Vodafone’s systems were showing that the area was covered, I received no proper explanation. I was repeatedly told that no service is available at that area and that nobody could do anything about it. When I asked for the matter to be escalated, I was initially told that even a manager could not help because no service at that area. After I ended the call, Mariam called me back and then suddenly offered to escalate the matter. This contradiction left me with very little confidence in the advice I was being given. And the managercall back time is 24hrs and that will add an extra day to the 30 days notice.

The most frustrating part is that I never asked to cancel my broadband. My intention was always to transfer my existing service to my new home. Instead of helping me do that, Vodafone told me they would cancel my service without charging an early termination fee, but only after a 30-day notice period.

This means I am now expected to pay for another month’s broadband service at an address where I no longer live and cannot use the service. I have already moved out of my previous property. Why should I pay for broadband that I cannot use simply because Vodafone cannot provide a clear and consistent answer about service availability at my new address?

I relied on Vodafone’s coverage checker before moving. I relied on the information provided by Vodafone’s chat support. I relied on Openreach’s availability checker, which clearly shows Full Fibre availability and Vodafone as a provider. At every stage, the information suggested that transferring my broadband would be possible.

Instead, I have been left with conflicting information, poor customer service, no clear explanation, and a demand to continue paying for another month of broadband that I cannot use.

The customer service I received was extremely disappointing. Rather than trying to help resolve the issue, I felt as though I was being pushed towards cancellation and expected to accept paying another month’s bill despite being unable to use the service.

I am requesting that Vodafone:

* Investigate why my address is being treated differently from neighbouring properties.
* Explain why Openreach shows Vodafone availability at my address while Vodafone claims it cannot provide service.
* Review the inaccurate and conflicting information provided by different Vodafone teams.
* Waive the 30-day notice period charges, as I have already moved out and cannot use the service.
* Provide a fair resolution to a situation that has arisen because of Vodafone’s own systems and information.

This experience has left me feeling misled, frustrated, and extremely disappointed. I trusted Vodafone’s coverage information when planning my move, and I believe customers deserve far better communication and support than what I have received.

LITERALLY the most incompetant company on the planet. their shop doesnt take cash! then, they cant even spell the name of their own company correctly, it says "voderfone" on their bills..
SO, im simply trying to pay my monthly bill. I firstly call them, enter all details, but it asks for a pin, which i dont have/remember. so i try using the app. it says itll text me a code to my phone which expires after 20 minutes. 25 minutes later i receive the code!!
THEN when i call and manage to speak to someone, i give all the details but she asks to speak to my mum the account holder. i take the phone to my mum who confirms im allowed to speak. then the vodafone lady is trying so many different ways and none of them are working. Then she keeps constantly interrupting me. im shouting at this point, saying "I JUST WANT TO PAY MY BILL!!! PLEASE, PLEASE TAKE MY MONEY"!, SHE THEN ASKS TO SPEAK TO THE ACCOUNT HOLDER AGAIN, I SAID "YOU JUST SPOKE TO HER A MINUTE AGO" eventually I said "you know what forget it, ill keep my money", and vodafone says at the start of the call about its partnership with "three" and says "2 networks are better than one"!!! not if the other network is as incompetant as you vodafone! they also have2 seperate bills for each of our phones, instead of simply having 1 bill for each phone! and the idiot in their store when i asked about paying my contract off early said to me "we keep the phone after you buy the contract out" to which i replied "ummm, no, no mobile provider does that"
stay away from this company, LITERALLY the most incompetant morons ive EVER come across in all my 48 years on this planet
EDIT:- I just tried to setup an online account on their website. it asked me to enter my number, which I did. it then said that wasnt a vodafone number, which it 100 percent was! so when i tried to use my email to set it up, as the laternate option i was presented with the popup "something went wrong, please contact us", oh my good lord.....
I cant wait for one of their scripted, disingenous responses to this review saying "oh we understand this is upsetting!" or try reaching out to us on social media, as if everyone is sad enough to use social media.....if a customer leaves a bad review, the reason theyre doing it isnt to get one of your dumb responses, its so you can fix your own company... thought that would be obvious

The service from Matthew at the Galleries outlet was first class. He was friendly, helpful and professional and did not "over sell". We were extremely happy with the mobile deals he provided for us and will probably return to change our broadband to Vodafone

Cynthia in sales is a most obnoxious individual with a strong accent. Despite many pleas for her to speak slowly and clearly or to pass me on to a native uk speaker she insisted on simply talking louder and faster in her version of "English".
She subjected me to half an hour of fast legalese each time abruptly demanding "Do you understand?"

overall the the whole experience was tainted by this one individual.

Should I decide to cancel the whole Vodafone experience, this will be due mainly to the attitude of this particular call centre droid.

Went on to on line help spent over an hour and that was a waste of time because nothing guy did helped.
So much for the ask once service. Because after about 3 hrs trying to sort a simple task out i am no further forward.
Kevin and Alex very helpful

A truly awful experience that I'd rather forget.

Let's start with their chat service, which is one of the worst I've ever used. It took over 30 minutes just to get through the security questions.

I contacted them last Friday because I had no signal, and I was assured that everything would be resolved within 2–3 days. Four days later, nothing has changed. Every time I call, they seem completely incapable of explaining what is actually happening. They keep talking about an "investigation" and repeatedly tell me that an engineer will visit.

I was told an engineer would come on 1st June. And do you know what happened? Nobody showed up.

The only response they seem capable of giving is: "It's not our fault... there's an outage." Then it's the same answer again: "There's an outage."

No explanation, no updates, no accountability.

At this point, I believe the mistake was mine for choosing Vodafone in the first place—a mistake I will never make again.
